design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character class with distinct playstyle, progress through interconnected hub-based levels, gain experience to increase stats (HP/mana), defeat enemies and bosses across themed continents to liberate world from Serpent Rider overlord
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Class-based asymmetry: players choose between offensive (Paladin), defensive (Crusader), spell-casting (Necromancer), or stealthy (Assassin) approaches; progression via stat growth creates power fantasy while maintaining challenge through level design and boss encounters
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Action-focused liberation narrative—free enslaved worlds from supernatural tyranny by mastering one of four distinct combat identities; progression fantasy through experience-based stat growth (HP, mana increases)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong class identity differentiation enforces distinct playstyles rather than convergent builds; hub-system design philosophy creates environmental interconnection where level changes affect other areas; medieval-fantasy-with-RPG-mechanics approach distinguishes it from pure action shooters like Doom/Quake; narrative frames combat as sequential continental liberation rather than arena progression

difficulty.checkpointing: Hub-based checkpoint system; interconnected levels within hubs allow non-linear navigation but progression tied to defeating area bosses/generals
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character class selection creates inherent difficulty variance (defensive Crusader vs. offensive Paladin affect survivability/damage output); experience system allows optional grinding/stat increases before facing bosses; Tome of Power artifact provides temporary power boost

progression.upgrade_system: Experience-based stat growth system: defeating enemies grants experience, increasing character HP and mana pools; each class has unique weapons that presumably scale or unlock with progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Hub structure with interconnected levels; progression through four continental hubs (Blackmarsh, Mazaera, Thysis, Septimus) unlocks access to next area; defeating four generals (tied to Four Horsemen reference) gates access to final Eidolon confrontation in Blackmarsh Cathedral
replayability_hooks: Four distinct character classes encourage replays with different playstyles
Hub-based level design with interconnected outcomes suggests different routing/puzzle solutions per class
Multiplayer modes (mentioned but not detailed in source)
Expansion pack Portal of Praevus adds fifth continent and Demoness class variant

world_building_techniques: Environmental theming: four continents with distinct architectural/cultural identities (Medieval European Blackmarsh, Mesoamerican Mazaera, Ancient Egyptian Thysis, Greco-Roman Septimus)
Narrative framing: world liberation against three Serpent Rider overlords across trilogy; Eidolon positioned as 'oldest and most powerful' final threat
Boss naming/symbolism: four generals reference Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse, providing mythic narrative weight
Hub-based world coherence: level changes in one area affect others, reinforcing narrative of environmental liberation
Expansion narrative continuation: Portal of Praevus explores attempted resurrection plot, adding thematic depth

boss_design.philosophy: Four-phase encounter structure (four continental generals) culminating in final confrontation with Eidolon in his Cathedral dominion; boss design tied to world liberation narrative arc; each general controls distinct thematic region
notable_bosses: Eidolon (primary antagonist, oldest/most powerful Serpent Rider, encountered in Cathedral final domain)
Four Generals (supporting bosses, one per continent, serve as gatekeepers to progression)

faction_indicators: Class-based tribal identities: Paladin (offensive/martial), Crusader (defensive/protective), Necromancer (dark arts), Assassin (stealth/subterfuge) suggest different guild/faction philosophies
Continental allegiances: progression through four distinct cultural regions (European, Mesoamerican, Egyptian, Greco-Roman) implies varied cultural identity markers
danger_signals: Serpent Rider tyranny framing: narrative establishes clear good vs. evil (liberation vs. enslavement) axis
General-as-warlord structure: four military leaders suggest despotic occupation requiring resistance
Cathedral dominion: Eidolon's final stronghold positions as authoritarian seat of power
environmental_storytelling: Themed continental architecture communicates cultural identity and occupier influence
Interconnected hub levels suggest infrastructure of oppression/control across world
Level changes affecting other areas implies cause-and-effect resistance impact (freeing one area aids liberation elsewhere)
